---
title: "Why Vulnerability MTTR Alone Misleads"
subtitle: "Add MOVA to Measure Real Risk"
author: "Caleb Kinney"
date: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import polars as pl
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_blank,
    element_line,
    element_rect,
    element_text,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    geom_text,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_color_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)

base_vulns = pl.read_parquet("data/base_vulns.parquet")
metrics = pl.read_parquet("data/metrics.parquet")
summary = pl.read_parquet("data/summary.parquet")

STRATEGY_LABELS = {
    "oldest_first": "Oldest-First",
    "newest_first": "Newest-First",
}
METRIC_COLORS = {"MTTR": "#1F6F78", "MOVA": "#C65A21"}
STRATEGY_COLORS = {
    "Oldest-First": "#1F6F78",
    "Newest-First": "#8B1E3F",
}
ROOM_BG = "#f6f0e5"
PANEL_BG = "#fffaf2"
GRID_COLOR = "#d9cfbf"


def fmt_days(value: float) -> str:
    return f"{value:.1f}"


metrics_labeled = metrics.with_columns(
    pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("strategy_label"),
    pl.col("month").dt.strftime("%b %Y").alias("month_label"),
)

plot_metrics = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        pl.col("mttr_days").alias("MTTR"),
        pl.col("mova_days").alias("MOVA"),
    )
    .unpivot(
        index=["strategy", "month_index"],
        on=["MTTR", "MOVA"],
        variable_name="metric",
        value_name="days",
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

newest_plot = plot_metrics[plot_metrics["strategy"] == "Newest-First"].copy()
oldest_plot = plot_metrics[plot_metrics["strategy"] == "Oldest-First"].copy()

tail_plot = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        "aged_over_180",
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

card_rows = (
    summary
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("strategy"),
        pl.col("mttr_days").round(1),
        pl.col("mova_days").round(1),
    )
    .sort("strategy", descending=True)
    .select("strategy", "mttr_days", "mova_days", "open_count", "aged_over_180")
    .to_dicts()
)

card_lookup = {row["strategy"]: row for row in card_rows}
oldest = card_lookup["Oldest-First"]
newest = card_lookup["Newest-First"]

oldest_mttr = fmt_days(oldest["mttr_days"])
oldest_mova = fmt_days(oldest["mova_days"])
oldest_open = int(oldest["open_count"])
oldest_tail = int(oldest["aged_over_180"])

newest_mttr = fmt_days(newest["mttr_days"])
newest_mova = fmt_days(newest["mova_days"])
newest_open = int(newest["open_count"])
newest_tail = int(newest["aged_over_180"])

shared_theme = (
    theme_minimal(base_size=22)
    + theme(
        figure_size=(11.2, 5.15),
        legend_position="top",
        legend_title=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_line(color=GRID_COLOR, size=0.55),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill=PANEL_BG, color=None),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill=ROOM_BG, color=None),
        axis_title=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        axis_text=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        plot_title=element_text(weight="bold", color="#101828"),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(color="#55606B", margin={"b": 10}),
        legend_text=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        plot_margin=0.16,
    )
)

chart_theme = shared_theme + theme(
    legend_position="none",
    axis_title_x=element_text(margin={"t": 10}),
    axis_title_y=element_text(margin={"r": 10}),
)

tail_theme = shared_theme + theme(
    legend_position="none",
    axis_title_x=element_text(margin={"t": 10}),
    axis_title_y=element_text(margin={"r": 10}),
)


def strategy_chart(source: pd.DataFrame, title: str, subtitle: str):
    labels = source[source["month_index"] == source["month_index"].max()].copy()
    labels["label"] = labels.apply(
        lambda row: f'{row["metric"]} {row["days"]:.0f}d', axis=1
    )

    return (
        ggplot(source, aes(x="month_index", y="days", color="metric"))
        + geom_line(size=2.35)
        + geom_point(data=labels, size=3.8)
        + geom_text(
            data=labels,
            mapping=aes(label="label"),
            ha="left",
            nudge_x=0.45,
            size=10,
        )
        + scale_color_manual(values=METRIC_COLORS)
        + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 26))
        + labs(title=title, subtitle=subtitle, x="Month", y="Days")
        + chart_theme
    )


tail_labels = tail_plot[tail_plot["month_index"] == tail_plot["month_index"].max()].copy()
tail_labels["label"] = tail_labels.apply(
    lambda row: f'{row["strategy"]} {row["aged_over_180"]}', axis=1
)

timeline_js = (
    metrics_labeled
    .filter(pl.col("strategy") == "oldest_first")
    .select(
        "month_index",
        "month_label",
        pl.col("month").dt.epoch(time_unit="ms").alias("month_ms"),
    )
    .sort("month_index")
    .to_dicts()
)

base_vulns_js = (
    base_vulns
    .select(
        "id",
        pl.col("created_at").dt.epoch(time_unit="ms").alias("created_ms"),
    )
    .to_dicts()
)
```

## MTTR doubled {.center .hook-slide}

:::: {.shock-card}
::: {.shock-label}
Mean time to remediate
:::

:::: {.shock-row}
::: {.shock-panel}
::: {.shock-caption}
Last month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
15d
:::
:::

::: {.shock-arrow}
→
:::

::: {.shock-panel .danger}
::: {.shock-caption}
This month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
31d
:::
:::
::::

::: {.shock-subtitle}
Most dashboards call this failure before they ask a better question.
:::
::::

---

## Why dashboards panic

::: {.incremental}
- Something slowed down
- The team got worse
- Leadership should worry
- Somebody is about to schedule a meeting about the red number
:::

---

## The paradox {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR can rise

while exposure drops
:::

::: {.callout}
Dashboards do not lie. They omit professionally.
:::

::: {.kicker .centered}
Same team. Same capacity. Better prioritization.
:::

---

## MTTR is flow

Time to remediate **closed** vulnerabilities.

::: {.kicker}
Useful for understanding closure speed. Blind to what is still open.
:::

---

## MOVA is stock

::: {.metric-mid}
**Mean Open Vulnerability Age**
:::

How old is the risk we still carry?

::: {.kicker}
MOVA measures the backlog that remains, not the work that already left the queue.
:::

---

## Flow vs. stock

::: {.definition-grid}
::: {.definition-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Flow
:::
### MTTR
How fast are we closing finished work?
:::

::: {.definition-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Stock
:::
### MOVA
How old is the backlog we still own?
:::
:::

---

## Averages hide the tail

::: {.tail-example-grid}
::: {.tail-card}
::: {.tail-number}
95
:::
::: {.tail-text}
findings at 7 days
:::
:::

::: {.tail-card}
::: {.tail-number}
5
:::
::: {.tail-text}
findings at 400 days
:::
:::

::: {.tail-card .emphasis}
::: {.tail-text .strong}
The average looks fine.
:::
::: {.tail-text}
The tail does not.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Report the bundle

::: {.signal-grid}
::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
MTTR
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Closure speed
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
MOVA
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Current exposure age
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
Open Count
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Backlog size
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
180+ Days
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Tail risk
:::
:::
:::

---

## Same records, different filter

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**MTTR**

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
mttr = (
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_not_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("resolved_at") - pl.col("created_at"))
        .dt.total_days()
        .alias("days_to_fix")
    )
    .select(pl.col("days_to_fix").mean().alias("mttr_days"))
)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**MOVA**

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
as_of = datetime(2026, 4, 1)

mova = (
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.lit(as_of) - pl.col("created_at"))
        .dt.total_days()
        .alias("open_age_days")
    )
    .select(pl.col("open_age_days").mean().alias("mova_days"))
)
```
:::
::::

::: {.kicker}
Same dataset. Different question. MTTR looks backward at closed work. MOVA looks at current exposure.
:::

---

## Quarto keeps the story reproducible

::: {.pipeline-grid}
::: {.pipeline-card}
::: {.pipeline-step}
01
:::
::: {.pipeline-title}
Generate
:::
::: {.pipeline-text}
`scripts/01_generate_data.py`
:::
:::

::: {.pipeline-card}
::: {.pipeline-step}
02
:::
::: {.pipeline-title}
Simulate
:::
::: {.pipeline-text}
`scripts/02_simulate_metrics.py`
:::
:::

::: {.pipeline-card}
::: {.pipeline-step}
03
:::
::: {.pipeline-title}
Build Outputs
:::
::: {.pipeline-text}
`scripts/03_build_outputs.py`
:::
:::

::: {.pipeline-card}
::: {.pipeline-step}
04
:::
::: {.pipeline-title}
Render Deck
:::
::: {.pipeline-text}
`index.qmd` + Reveal.js + OJS
:::
:::
:::

::: {.kicker}
Python generates the reproducible figures. Observable JS handles the interactive simulation. Quarto keeps both in one file.
:::

---

## Hold capacity constant

::: {.simulation-grid}
::: {.sim-card}
::: {.sim-number}
24
:::
::: {.sim-label}
months
:::
:::

::: {.sim-card}
::: {.sim-number}
360
:::
::: {.sim-label}
starting backlog
:::
:::

::: {.sim-card}
::: {.sim-number}
60
:::
::: {.sim-label}
new / month
:::
:::

::: {.sim-card}
::: {.sim-number}
60
:::
::: {.sim-label}
fixed / month
:::
:::
:::

::: {.kicker}
Same arrivals. Same fix rate. Same time horizon. Only prioritization changes.
:::

---

## Only the order changes

::: {.strategy-grid}
::: {.strategy-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Strategy A
:::
### Newest-First
Fix newest findings first.
:::

::: {.strategy-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Strategy B
:::
### Oldest-First
Fix oldest findings first.
:::
:::

::: {.kicker}
MTTR in the simulation is cumulative average days-to-close for everything closed so far.
:::

---

## Step through the simulation

```{python}
#| echo: false
ojs_define(
    base_vulns_js=base_vulns_js,
    timeline_js=timeline_js,
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
dayMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000

strategyMeta = [
  {key: "oldest_first", label: "Oldest-First", color: "#1F6F78"},
  {key: "newest_first", label: "Newest-First", color: "#8B1E3F"}
]

strategyLabel = new Map(strategyMeta.map((d) => [d.key, d.label]))

monthTimeline = timeline_js.map((d) => ({
  monthIndex: d.month_index,
  monthLabel: d.month_label,
  asOf: new Date(d.month_ms)
}))

baseRecords = base_vulns_js.map((d) => ({
  id: d.id,
  createdAt: new Date(d.created_ms),
  resolvedAt: null
}))

mean = (values) =>
  values.length
    ? values.reduce((sum, value) => sum + value, 0) / values.length
    : NaN

daysBetween = (later, earlier) => (later - earlier) / dayMs

activeVulns = (vulns, asOf) => vulns.filter((vuln) => vuln.createdAt <= asOf)

selectToFix = (vulns, limit, strategy) =>
  vulns
    .filter((vuln) => vuln.resolvedAt === null)
    .slice()
    .sort((left, right) =>
      strategy === "newest_first"
        ? right.createdAt - left.createdAt
        : left.createdAt - right.createdAt
    )
    .slice(0, limit)

simulateStrategy = (strategy, monthlyFix = 60) => {
  const backlog = baseRecords.map((vuln) => ({...vuln}))
  const snapshots = []

  for (const month of monthTimeline) {
    const visible = activeVulns(backlog, month.asOf)
    const selected = selectToFix(visible, monthlyFix, strategy)
    const selectedIds = new Set(selected.map((vuln) => vuln.id))

    for (const vuln of backlog) {
      if (selectedIds.has(vuln.id) && vuln.resolvedAt === null) {
        vuln.resolvedAt = month.asOf
      }
    }

    const current = activeVulns(backlog, month.asOf)
    const open = current.filter((vuln) => vuln.resolvedAt === null)
    const closed = backlog.filter(
      (vuln) => vuln.resolvedAt !== null && vuln.resolvedAt <= month.asOf
    )

    const openAges = open.map((vuln) => daysBetween(month.asOf, vuln.createdAt))
    const closedDurations = closed.map((vuln) =>
      daysBetween(vuln.resolvedAt, vuln.createdAt)
    )

    snapshots.push({
      strategy,
      strategyLabel: strategyLabel.get(strategy),
      monthIndex: month.monthIndex,
      monthLabel: month.monthLabel,
      mttrDays: mean(closedDurations),
      movaDays: mean(openAges),
      openCount: open.length,
      closedCount: closed.length,
      agedOver180: openAges.filter((days) => days >= 180).length
    })
  }

  return snapshots
}

simMetrics = strategyMeta.flatMap((strategy) => simulateStrategy(strategy.key))

currentRows = (monthIndex) =>
  simMetrics.filter((row) => row.monthIndex === monthIndex)

currentLookup = (monthIndex) =>
  new Map(currentRows(monthIndex).map((row) => [row.strategyLabel, row]))

simCardHtml = (monthIndex) => {
  const rows = currentLookup(monthIndex)
  const newest = rows.get("Newest-First")
  const oldest = rows.get("Oldest-First")

  return html`
    <div class="dashboard-grid">
      <div class="dashboard-card dashboard-detail">
        <div class="dashboard-title">Newest-First</div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>MTTR</span><strong>${newest.mttrDays.toFixed(1)} days</strong></div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>MOVA</span><strong>${newest.movaDays.toFixed(1)} days</strong></div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>Open</span><strong>${newest.openCount}</strong></div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>180+ days</span><strong>${newest.agedOver180}</strong></div>
      </div>
      <div class="dashboard-card dashboard-detail winner">
        <div class="dashboard-title">Oldest-First</div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>MTTR</span><strong>${oldest.mttrDays.toFixed(1)} days</strong></div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>MOVA</span><strong>${oldest.movaDays.toFixed(1)} days</strong></div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>Open</span><strong>${oldest.openCount}</strong></div>
        <div class="dashboard-stat"><span>180+ days</span><strong>${oldest.agedOver180}</strong></div>
      </div>
    </div>
  `
}

comparisonSentence = (monthIndex) => {
  const rows = currentLookup(monthIndex)
  const newest = rows.get("Newest-First")
  const oldest = rows.get("Oldest-First")

  return html`
    <div class="sim-callout">
      <strong>${monthTimeline[monthIndex - 1].monthLabel}:</strong>
      Newest-First keeps MTTR lower at <strong>${newest.mttrDays.toFixed(1)} days</strong>,
      but Oldest-First cuts MOVA to <strong>${oldest.movaDays.toFixed(1)} days</strong>
      while Newest-First still carries <strong>${newest.agedOver180}</strong> findings older than 180 days.
    </div>
  `
}

metricPlot = ({metric, label, width = 430}) =>
  Plot.plot({
    width,
    height: 255,
    marginTop: 34,
    marginRight: 18,
    marginBottom: 44,
    marginLeft: 64,
    style: {
      background: "#fffaf2",
      color: "#182126",
      fontFamily: "Avenir Next, Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif",
      fontSize: "14px"
    },
    color: {
      legend: true,
      domain: strategyMeta.map((d) => d.label),
      range: strategyMeta.map((d) => d.color)
    },
    x: {label: "Month", ticks: [1, 6, 12, 18, 24]},
    y: {label, grid: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleX([selectedMonth], {stroke: "#c65a21", strokeWidth: 2}),
      Plot.lineY(simMetrics, {
        x: "monthIndex",
        y: metric,
        stroke: "strategyLabel",
        strokeWidth: 4
      }),
      Plot.dot(
        simMetrics.filter((row) => row.monthIndex === selectedMonth),
        {
          x: "monthIndex",
          y: metric,
          fill: "strategyLabel",
          r: 5
        }
      ),
      Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#d9cfbf"})
    ]
  })

tailPlot = (width = 620) =>
  Plot.plot({
    width,
    height: 305,
    marginTop: 34,
    marginRight: 18,
    marginBottom: 44,
    marginLeft: 64,
    style: {
      background: "#fffaf2",
      color: "#182126",
      fontFamily: "Avenir Next, Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif",
      fontSize: "14px"
    },
    color: {
      legend: true,
      domain: strategyMeta.map((d) => d.label),
      range: strategyMeta.map((d) => d.color)
    },
    x: {label: "Month", ticks: [1, 6, 12, 18, 24]},
    y: {label: "Open vulnerabilities older than 180 days", grid: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleX([selectedMonth], {stroke: "#c65a21", strokeWidth: 2}),
      Plot.lineY(simMetrics, {
        x: "monthIndex",
        y: "agedOver180",
        stroke: "strategyLabel",
        strokeWidth: 4
      }),
      Plot.dot(
        simMetrics.filter((row) => row.monthIndex === selectedMonth),
        {
          x: "monthIndex",
          y: "agedOver180",
          fill: "strategyLabel",
          r: 5
        }
      ),
      Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#d9cfbf"})
    ]
  })

simulationControl = ({labels, initialValue = 1}) => {
  const form = html`
    <form class="sim-controls">
      <button type="button">Play</button>
      <input type="range" min="1" max="${labels.length}" step="1" value="${initialValue}" />
      <div class="sim-status"></div>
    </form>
  `

  const button = form.querySelector("button")
  const slider = form.querySelector("input")
  const status = form.querySelector(".sim-status")
  let timer = null

  const stop = () => {
    if (timer !== null) {
      clearInterval(timer)
      timer = null
    }
    button.textContent = "Play"
  }

  const update = (emit = true) => {
    form.value = Number(slider.value)
    status.textContent = `Month ${form.value} of ${labels.length} • ${labels[form.value - 1]}`
    if (emit) {
      form.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", {bubbles: true}))
    }
  }

  slider.addEventListener("input", () => update())
  button.addEventListener("click", (event) => {
    event.preventDefault()
    if (timer !== null) {
      stop()
      return
    }

    button.textContent = "Pause"
    timer = setInterval(() => {
      slider.value = Number(slider.value) >= labels.length ? 1 : Number(slider.value) + 1
      update()
    }, 1200)
  })

  update(false)
  invalidation.then(stop)
  return form
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedMonth = simulationControl({
  labels: monthTimeline.map((month) => month.monthLabel),
  initialValue: 1
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
comparisonSentence(selectedMonth)
```

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metricPlot({metric: "mttrDays", label: "MTTR (days)"})
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metricPlot({metric: "movaDays", label: "MOVA (days)"})
```
:::
::::

::: {.kicker}
Use the slider or Play to watch the paradox emerge month by month.
:::

---

## The tail appears slowly

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="42%"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
simCardHtml(selectedMonth)
```
:::

::: {.column width="58%"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
tailPlot()
```
:::
::::

::: {.kicker}
Newest-First keeps the closure metric pretty. Oldest-First removes the long-lived exposure that leadership actually owns.
:::

---

## Newest-First wins MTTR

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart for newest-first prioritization. Cumulative MTTR stays low while MOVA climbs sharply over the 24-month period."
strategy_chart(
    newest_plot,
    title="Newest-First",
    subtitle="Cumulative MTTR stays low while the open backlog keeps aging",
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
Fast closures. Old risk.
:::

---

## Oldest-First lowers exposure

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart for oldest-first prioritization. MTTR rises because old work closes, while MOVA drops as old exposure leaves the backlog."
strategy_chart(
    oldest_plot,
    title="Oldest-First",
    subtitle="MTTR rises because old work closes; MOVA falls because old risk disappears",
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
Higher MTTR. Lower exposure.
:::

---

## The 180+ tail tells the truth

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart comparing the count of open vulnerabilities older than 180 days for oldest-first and newest-first prioritization."
(
    ggplot(tail_plot, aes(x="month_index", y="aged_over_180", color="strategy"))
    + geom_line(size=2.35)
    + geom_point(data=tail_labels, size=3.8)
    + geom_text(
        data=tail_labels,
        mapping=aes(label="label"),
        ha="left",
        nudge_x=0.45,
        size=10,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(values=STRATEGY_COLORS)
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 26))
    + labs(
        title="180+ day backlog",
        subtitle="Newest-First keeps the tail alive",
        x="Month",
        y="Open vulnerabilities",
    )
    + tail_theme
)
```

---

## End state: the tail is the story

::: {.score-grid .tail-grid}
::: {.score-card .score-good}
::: {.score-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.score-metric}
`{python} oldest_tail`
:::
::: {.score-label}
findings older than 180 days
:::
:::

::: {.score-card .score-bad}
::: {.score-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.score-metric}
`{python} newest_tail`
:::
::: {.score-label}
findings older than 180 days
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
Under newest-first, every open item is older than 180 days.
:::

---

## Leadership sees MTTR only {auto-animate="true"}

::: {.dashboard-grid}
::: {.dashboard-card .winner}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-value}
`{python} newest_mttr` days
:::
::: {.dashboard-label}
MTTR
:::
:::

::: {.dashboard-card}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-value}
`{python} oldest_mttr` days
:::
::: {.dashboard-label}
MTTR
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
If this is the only KPI, teams will close tickets like it’s cardio.
:::

---

## Leadership sees the full system {auto-animate="true"}

::: {.dashboard-grid}
::: {.dashboard-card .dashboard-detail}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MTTR</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_mttr` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MOVA</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_mova` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>Open</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_open`</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>180+ days</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_tail`</strong>
:::
:::

::: {.dashboard-card .dashboard-detail .winner}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MTTR</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_mttr` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MOVA</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_mova` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>Open</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_open`</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>180+ days</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_tail`</strong>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
MTTR still matters. It just stops being the only story.
:::

---

## Read the signals together

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR rising + MOVA falling = backlog cleanup
- MTTR falling + MOVA rising = optics improving while exposure worsens
- 180+ rising = risk is getting stranded in the tail
:::

---

## Reporting guidance

::: {.incremental}
- Put MTTR and MOVA on the same monthly dashboard
- Add open count and an aged backlog threshold like 180+ days
- Label the MTTR window clearly before you interpret a regression
- Use the bundle to explain cleanup periods when old backlog finally gets closed
:::

---

## Final line {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR measures closure speed.

MOVA measures exposure remaining.

You need both.
:::
